title: Itemgroup is mandatory on every data row
description: Every survey row whose type is a data type (text, integer, decimal, date, time, dateTime, select_one, select_multiple, note, calculate) must have bind__oc_itemgroup populated with a short group code. The code uses letters, digits, and underscores only, must not start with a digit, and must not contain a period. begin/end group and begin/end repeat rows do not need this field. EXCEPTION (per OC-5a): calculate rows with bind__oc_external='clinicaldata' must NOT have an itemgroup — external lookups read from elsewhere and do not persist locally.
rationale: OC4 uses bind__oc_itemgroup to organize data items into logical groups for analysis and export. A row without an itemgroup, or with an itemgroup containing a period or starting with a digit, causes silent OC rejection at form upload — the form appears to load but the row is dropped from the active definition.

flag.message: Field ${field.name} has invalid bind__oc_itemgroup value '${field.bind__oc_itemgroup}'. Must use letters/digits/underscores only, must not start with a digit, must not contain a period.

history.note: Imported from prompts.py RULE OC-2 during Phase B.1a. The require directive enforces presence; the flag directive (if extended to support a value-validation predicate) would enforce format. Format-validation currently lives in build_xlsforms validation — Phase B/C may extend the DSL to express the regex check declaratively.
